xe.d: n a colorless odorless inert gaseous element occurring in the earth's atmosphere in trace amounts

xebec.d:
n. A small three-masted vessel, with projecting bow stern and
   convex decks, used in the Mediterranean for transporting merchandise,
   etc. It carries large square sails, or both. Xebecs were formerly armed
   and used by corsairs.

xedit.t: [计] 系统产品扩展编辑程序

xel.t: abbr. extensible engineer language 可扩展工程师语言（代号）; XeCl excimer laser 氯化氙准分子激光器

xeme.d: n. An Arctic fork-tailed gull (Xema Sabinii).

xen.t: abbr. Xenia 种子直感，异粉性; Xenos （希腊语）陌生（的）

xen-.t:
pref. 外来的；陌生的；异质的（等于xeno-）
n. (Xen)人名；(老)森

xenazoic acid.t: [化] 珍那佐酸; 联苯酰胺苯酸

xenelasia.d:
n. A Spartan institution which prohibited strangers from
   residing in Sparta without permission, its object probably being to
   preserve the national simplicity of manners.

xenenthesis.t: [医] 异物导入, 异物侵入

xenicus.t: [网络] 异鹩属；鹪鹩类；刺鹩属

xenium.d:
n. A present given to a guest or stranger, or to a foreign
   ambassador.

xenix.t: [计] 以UNIX为基础的操作系统

xeno.t: abbr. xenophobe 仇外; xenophobia 仇外; xenogenic 异种的; xenolithic 外来石的

xeno-.t: 表示“陌生人”,“外国人”,“外来的”

xenoantiserum.t: n. [生](异种抗体中富有的) 异种抗血清

xenobiology.t: n. 外(层)空(间)生物学,宇宙生物学

xenobiotic.t: n. & adj.(药物、杀虫剂、致癌物等)异型生物质(的)

xenocentric.t: a. 喜爱外国之文化的

xenocrates.t: n. 齐诺克雷蒂（古希腊哲学家）

xenocurrency.t: n. (在原发行国域外流通的)国外货币

xenodocheionology.t: 关于旅馆、客栈方面的经验知识

xenodochium.t: n. (中世纪时收容香客,异乡人,病人的)救济院
xenodochium.d:
n. A house for the reception of strangers.
n. In the Middle Ages, a room in a monastery for the
   reception and entertainment of strangers and pilgrims, and for the
   relief of paupers. [Called also Xenodocheion.]

xenogeneic.t: a. [生]异种的, 异基因的

n. 自然发生, 世代交替, 亲子异型
[医] 世代交替, 异配生殖, 亲子异形
xenogenesis.d: n the alternation of two or more different forms in the life cycle of a plant or animal

xenogenetic.t: a. 自然发生的, 世代交替的

a. Of or pertaining to xenogenesis; as, the xenogenetic
   origin of microzymes.

xenogenous.t: 由体外原因发生的, 体外性的, 异体的

xenogeny.t: n. [生物] 自然发生, 世代交替

xenoglossia.t: n. (自称具有的)讲陌生语言的能力

xenograft.d: n tissue from an animal of one species used as a temporary graft (as in cases of severe burns) on an individual of another species

xenografts.d:
plural of xenograft
n tissue from an animal of one species used as a temporary graft (as in cases of severe burns) on an individual of another species

xenolalia.t: n. (自称具有的)讲陌生语言的能力

plural of xenolith
n. (geology) a piece of rock of different origin from the igneous rock in which it is embedded

xenomania.t: n. 外国迷, 外国狂

n. A mania for, or an inordinate attachment to, foreign
   customs, institutions, manners, fashions, etc.

xenomenia.t: [医]代偿性月经, 异位月经

xenon.d: n. a colorless odorless inert gaseous element occurring in the earth's atmosphere in trace amounts

[法] 仇视外国人, 恐惧外国人者, 害怕生人者

n. 无端畏惧和憎恨外国人/陌生人, 对外国人的畏惧和憎恨
[医] 生客恐怖
xenophobia.d: n. a fear of foreigners or strangers

xenophobic.t:
a. 畏惧和憎恨外国人的
[法] 仇外的, 恐惧外国人的
xenophobic.d: s. suffering from xenophobia; having abnormal fear or hatred of the strange or foreign

[法] 仇外的, 恐惧外国人的

plural of xenophobic
s. suffering from xenophobia; having abnormal fear or hatred of the strange or foreign

xenophon.d: n. Greek general and historian; student of Socrates (430-355 BC)

xenophons.t: (Xenophon 的复数) 色诺芬(约公元前434-前355, 希腊将军, 历史学家, 著有《长征记》一书)
xenophons.d:
plural of Xenophon
n. Greek general and historian; student of Socrates (430-355 BC)

xenotime.d: n a brown-to-yellow mineral that is a phosphate of yttrium in crystalline form

xenotransplant.d: n. a surgical procedure in which tissue or whole organs are transfered from one species to another species

xenotransplantation.d: n. a surgical procedure in which tissue or whole organs are transfered from one species to another species

[化] 联苯基

xer.d: s. being one more than nine

xeric.t: a. 干旱的, 环境干燥的, 耐旱的, 沙漠般的

xerophile.d: n plant adapted for life with a limited supply of water; compare hydrophyte and mesophyte

xerophilous.t: a. 适旱的, 好旱的, 旱栖的

a. Drought-loving; able withstand the absence or lack of
   moisture.

xerophobia.t: [医] 恐怖性唾液分泌停止

n. 眼干燥, 干眼病

xerophytic.d: s adapted to a xeric (or dry) environment

n. 干燥病
[医] 干燥[病]

xerotes.d: n the condition of not containing or being covered by a liquid (especially water)
